questions.question: The Part IX of the Indian Constitution deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: It specifically focuses on the Panchayats.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is specifically mentioned under Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution?
options: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression
questions.explanation: It is the freedom of speech and expression.

questions.question: The Panchayats in West Bengal are governed by which of the following Acts?
options: The Panchayats Extension of Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act, 1996
questions.explanation: It is the WB Panchayat Act of 2003.

questions.question: The concept of which of the following is mentioned under Article 14 of the Indian Constitution?
options: Protection of the right to life
Right to freedom of speech and expression
questions.explanation: It is the equality before the law.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary function of the West Bengal State Election Commission?
questions.explanation: It conducts all the state elections.

questions.question: Which of the following movements led to the partition of Bengal in 1905?
questions.explanation: The partition of Bengal was a result of the Swadeshi Movement and the Partition Movement.

questions.question: Who was the President of the Indian National Congress at the time of the Non-Cooperation Movement in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Satish Chandra Mukherjee was the President of the Indian National Congress at the time of the Non-Cooperation Movement in Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following leaders was associated with the Bengal Volunteers in the Indian independence movement?
questions.explanation: Bipin Chandra Pal was associated with the Bengal Volunteers in the Indian independence movement.

questions.question: The Jugantar Party was a revolutionary organization formed in Bengal in the early 20th century. What was its main goal?
options: To overthrow the British government through armed struggle
To achieve independence through non-violent means
To reform the Indian National Congress
questions.explanation: The Jugantar Party's main goal was to overthrow the British government through armed struggle.

questions.question: Which of the following events marked the beginning of the Swadeshi Movement in Bengal?
options: The arrest of Rabindranath Tagore in 1908
questions.explanation: The partition of Bengal in 1905 marked the beginning of the Swadeshi Movement in Bengal.

questions.question: Which river forms the international border between West Bengal and Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: The Meghna River forms the border between India and Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges is located in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sikkim Himalayas are a major mountain range in West Bengal's Darjeeling district.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest mangrove forest in the world, located in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans is the largest mangrove forest in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates from the Gaurishankar range and flows through North Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Teesta River originates from the Gaurishankar range and flows through North Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following cities is the administrative headquarters of the Malda district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Malda is the administrative headquarters of the Malda district.

questions.question: The West Bengal government launched 'Disha' scheme in which year to ensure safety and security for women in the state?
questions.explanation: Launched in 2024.

questions.question: Which of the following river is the main source of water for the Sundarbans mangrove forest in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River is the main source of water for the Sundarbans.

questions.question: The West Bengal government announced a plan to develop which of the following as a major tourist hub in the state?
questions.explanation: Announced as a major tourist hub in 2026.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the new Chief Minister of West Bengal in May 2024?
questions.explanation: Re-elected as Chief Minister.

questions.question: The West Bengal government launched a new initiative to promote the use of which of the following in the state?
questions.explanation: CNG promotion initiative in 2025.

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an item at 15% profit. If he sold 100 items, and his total selling price is ₹ 70,000, then his total cost price is?
questions.explanation: Using the formula for profit percentage.

questions.question: If the price of a share falls by 20%, and its current market price is ₹ 80, then the original price of the share was?
questions.explanation: Using the formula for percentage decrease.

questions.question: A merchant marks an item 25% above the cost price and then allows a discount of 10% on the marked price. The selling price after the discount is ₹ 2,250. What is the cost price?
questions.explanation: Applying the concept of successive percentage changes.

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys 10 kg of potatoes at ₹ 12 per kg. He sells 5 kg of potatoes at ₹ 18 per kg. What is his net percentage profit or loss?
questions.explanation: Using the formula for percentage profit or loss.

questions.question: A trader sells two items, one at a 10% profit and the other at a 10% loss. If he sells both items for ₹ 2,400, the cost price of the item sold at a profit is?
questions.explanation: Using the concept of weighted average.

questions.question: A person deposits ₹ 5,000 for 2 years at 8% p.a. compound interest. What will be the amount after 2 years?
questions.explanation: Using compound interest formula A = P(1 + r/100)^n.

questions.question: What is the simple interest on ₹ 8,000 at 6% p.a. for 3 years?
questions.explanation: Using simple interest formula I = Prn/100.

questions.question: A sum of ₹ 10,000 is lent for 5 years at 5% p.a. simple interest. What is the interest earned?
questions.explanation: Using simple interest formula I = Prn/100.

questions.question: What is the compound interest on ₹ 7,000 at 9% p.a. for 2 years, compounded annually?
questions.explanation: Using compound interest formula A = P(1 + r/100)^n.

questions.question: Simplifying the following expression: (1 + 6/100)^2, the result is:
questions.explanation: Using exponent rules.

questions.question: A contractor has to finish a work in 140 days with a workforce of 60. However, due to some reason, he had to engage only 45 workers. How many days will he take to finish the work?
questions.explanation: The work remains the same, but the man-days decrease.

questions.question: A pipe can fill 1/4th of a tank in 6 minutes. In how many minutes can it fill the tank?
questions.explanation: The time to fill the tank is the reciprocal of the fraction of the tank filled per minute.

questions.question: A man can do a piece of work in 20 days. He worked at it for 5 days and then 2 more men joined him. How long will it take to finish the work?
questions.explanation: The work remains the same, but the man-days increase.

questions.question: A pipe can fill 1/3rd of a tank in 3 hours. How long will it take to fill 9/10th of the tank?
questions.explanation: The time to fill the tank is proportional to the fraction of the tank filled.

questions.question: A man can finish a piece of work in 5 days with the help of his son. If he works alone, how many days will it take to finish the work?
questions.explanation: The time to finish the work increases when the man works alone.

questions.question: A train covers a distance of 120 km between Howrah and Kolkata in 2 hours. How much speed of the train is required to cover the same distance in 3 hours?
questions.explanation: Speed of the train is required to be 60 km/h.

questions.question: A car travels from Siliguri to Jalpaiguri and covers a distance of 160 km in 4 hours. What is the average speed of the car?
questions.explanation: Average speed of the car is 40 km/h.

questions.question: A bus covers a distance of 240 km from Durgapur to Asansol in 6 hours. Find the speed of the bus?
questions.explanation: Speed of the bus is 40 km/h.

questions.question: A person travels from New Jalpaiguri to Alipurduar by car and covers a distance of 180 km in 5 hours. What is the average speed?
questions.explanation: Average speed of the car is 36 km/h.

questions.question: A train starts from Sealdah and reaches the destination at 10:30 AM after covering a distance of 150 km in 4.5 hours. What was the starting time of the train?
questions.explanation: Starting time of the train was 06:30 AM.

questions.question: Kolkata's Howrah Bridge is to the North-East of the Indian Statistical Institute, and the Ganges River is to the North-West. If you are facing the Indian Statistical Institute, which direction will the Hoogly River be in?
questions.explanation: As the Hoogly River is to the South of the Ganges River.

questions.question: A person is at a bus stop in the town of Barrackpore. He is facing towards the bank of the Kalindi River and notices that the town's Post Office is 3 blocks East and 2 blocks North of his current location. What direction is the Post Office in relation to the person?
questions.explanation: Post Office is 3 blocks East and 2 blocks North.

questions.question: You are in the city of Asansol and face the direction of the Damodar River. If you move 5 blocks North and then 2 blocks East, which direction will the Damodar River be in?
questions.explanation: After moving 5 blocks North and 2 blocks East.

questions.question: A person is at the Victoria Memorial in Kolkata and faces the direction of the Ganges River. If he moves 2 blocks East and then 4 blocks South, which direction will the Ganges River be in?
questions.explanation: After moving 2 blocks East and 4 blocks South.

questions.question: You are standing near the Nakhari Math in Bishnupur and face the direction of the Bishnupur Palace. If you move 3 blocks West and then 2 blocks North, which direction will the Bishnupur Palace be in?
questions.explanation: After moving 3 blocks West and 2 blocks North.

questions.question: If the pattern 3, 6, 12, 24, ? is continued, then the next number in the series would be?
questions.explanation: The pattern is obtained by multiplying the previous term by 2.

questions.question: If the pattern A, B, C, D, ? is continued, then the next letter in the series would be?
questions.explanation: The pattern is in alphabetical order.

questions.question: If the pattern 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, ? is continued, then the next number in the series would be?
questions.explanation: The pattern is obtained by adding consecutive prime numbers.

questions.question: If the pattern a, ab, abc, abcd, ? is continued, then the next term in the series would be?
questions.explanation: The pattern is obtained by adding the next letter 'E' in sequence.

questions.question: If the pattern 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, ? is continued, then the next number in the series would be?
questions.explanation: The pattern is obtained by adding 3 to the previous term.
